rows.description: Computus Gu: Paynter vris a 2do die novembris 1700 ad eundem diem 1701.
rows.notes: Clear page title at top centre. Handwriting legible and no amounts present.

rows.description: Imprimis reddit comput. de remanentib. in Cista
rows.notes: Numbers well aligned under the three right-hand ruling columns: '375' in pounds column, '16' in shillings, '09' in pence. All values within valid ranges and handwriting clear.
Clear reading '060 00 00' aligned in £, s, d columns. All values valid.

Right-hand group reads '028 01 39' but pence '39' is outside valid 0–11 range. The three numeric groups are clearly written but column alignment may be ambiguous here (possible stitch/ink blur). I recorded the visually read groups but pence likely mis-positioned or misread; low confidence for correct column separation.
Pounds read '058' clearly; shillings and pence read '00 00'. The place name is partially unclear (handwritten 'Longwitt...' ), so description has '?' to indicate uncertainty.
Amount reads '007 10 00' (7£ 10s 0d). Description is somewhat cramped but legible as 'minhinett & anno'. Alignment with columns is clear.
Amount reads '002 15 07' (2£ 15s 7d). Slight ink fade on pence '07' but alignment with column lines gives confidence.

Right-hand grouping appears as '001 9 79' on the page; pence reads '79' which violates pence range. The '79' likely results from mis-segmentation of the two pence digits from adjacent rows or a smudge. Recorded literal groups but assignment to pence is almost certainly incorrect; very low confidence.
The place name 'Bampton' is clearly present but no clear amount is written on the same line (or the amount is masked by an ink blot). Treated as an entry (sub-item) with no visible amount.

rows.description: montague &c (word partly struck-through) up triennio et dimidio
rows.notes: There is a crossed/overwritten line at this position; the text (Montague/word) is partly struck-through and any amount immediately to the right is faint/obscured. I treat it as an entry with no recoverable amount.
Amount reads '000 10 00' clearly. Description legible.
Amount '000 8 06' (0£ 8s 6d) aligned and legible.
Amount reads '004 13 04' (4£ 13s 4d). All three groups clear.
Amount appears as '000 10 00' under the columns; description legible.
Amount read as '007 8 4' — the last pence digit is smaller and slightly smudged; interpreted as 7£ 8s 4d. Slight uncertainty on the pence digit.

There is a brace grouping 'Tintonhill' with two subitems 'Rush' and 'Hopkins' (both 'sesquianno'). A single amount group adjacent reads '001 3 9' (1£ 3s 9d) but the lines for subitems are cramped; moderate ambiguity whether that amount applies to one or both subitems. Treated as an entry with the visible amount.
Amount reads '002 4 4' (2£ 4s 4d). Handwriting clear for the numbers and description.

Recorded as '001 15 11' (1£ 15s 11d). Pence '11' is legible but slightly darker/irregular — moderate confidence. There is an extra small mark after pence that might be a fraction but not clearly 'ob' or 'q'.
Description clear; there is a following longer explanatory line. No clear amount visible on same line (or amount is faint). Marked as entry with no amount.

rows.description: Trethewin pro triennio et dimidio, Rented in p't
rows.notes: Amount clearly reads '098 00 00' (98£ 0s 0d) and is aligned to the right columns; description contains abbreviation 'Recd in p't' which is legible.
Amount reads '001 14 00' (1£ 14s 0d). Text 'vic pro sesquianno' is slightly condensed but readable; numbers clearly aligned.
This line lists names 'Gent Com'oners Martin - Harris' with no obvious amount alongside. Treated as header-like entry introducing the next line but kept as entry because it's a transaction/note line.

Amount appears as '002 6 16' (the last two digits are cramped). I have read as '002 6 1' with a faint trailing mark — alignment is somewhat uncertain. There is ink smudge on the pence. Because of the smudge the pence digit is uncertain; moderate to low confidence.

rows.description: Southnewington tenement, 4 annos of which 3 & 4 for ye former year
rows.notes: Long explanatory line trailing across the page. No clear amount on the same line (or amount is extremely faint). Treated as entry/note.
Subtotal boxed on right reads '573 16 10 1/2' (halfpenny indicated by 'ob' style loop). Alignment and total format are clear.
Centered header for the receipts of the present year; clearly written and separates the lower section.
Single-word entry 'Guinear' with no amount visible on the line or amount is obscured by a shadow; treated as entry without recoverable amount.

Right-hand column for this row reads '28 17 6' (pounds, shillings, pence). The leftmost part of the description is faint but legible as 'Longwittenam - Ann'. Moderate confidence in description; numbers clearer.
Description present ('Minhinett') but the amount column for this line falls into the page shadow and is illegible; no amount recorded.

rows.description: Southnewington Ann: m p't 20s more in full for Ann. 1701
rows.notes: Amount reads '28 00 00' clearly. The annotation 'm p't 20s more in full for Ann.1701' is legible and written under the place name; alignment of the numeric group is clear.
Amount '20 00 00' is clear and aligned. Place name 'Luddington Rey' is legible.
Amount reads '25 00 00' (25£). Description 'Yardington Ann. St' legible.
Amount reads '03 3 00' — shillings '3' clear but pence faint. Interpreted as 3£ 3s 0d; moderate confidence due to faint pence.
Amount reads '02 10 00' clearly (2£ 10s). Description legible.
Amount '04 00 00' is clear. Description 'Shrup Ann' legible.
Amount reads '02 00 00' clearly aligned. Description legible.
Name 'Lucas Eaton' is visible as a final line in the receipts list but the amount is either off the cropped area or obscured by shadow; recorded as entry with no amount.
Final receipts total at bottom right reads '093 10 6 1/2' (halfpenny indicated). Numbers are boxed/underlined and clearly aligned; halfpenny mark legible as 'ob' style loop.
